questions.text: Which factors increase the complexity and risk of laparoscopic adhesiolysis? Select 3 best answers.

answerOptions.text: C) Dense cohesive adhesions to pelvic sidewall
E) Use of laparoscopic scissors for all cases

questions.explanation: Dense adhesions near bowel or major vessels carry higher risk of visceral or vascular injury. Filmy adhesions are low risk; scissors are safe when used selectively.

questions.text: Which of the following are appropriate management steps after salpingostomy for ectopic pregnancy? Select all that apply.

answerOptions.text: A) Administer intramuscular methotrexate if β-hCG remains elevated
B) Schedule follow-up ultrasound at 1 week post-op
C) Monitor β-hCG levels serially until undetectable
D) Repeat laparoscopy in 4 weeks to confirm tubal healing
E) Counsel about risk of persistent trophoblastic tissue

questions.explanation: Persistent trophoblastic tissue occurs in up to ~20% after salpingostomy. Serial β-hCG is mandatory; consider methotrexate if levels plateau or rise. Routine US or reoperation isn’t required unless concerns arise.
questions.submodule: Module 8.2 – Laparoscopic Treatment of Ectopic Pregnancy

questions.text: Which 2 operative techniques are most important for preserving ovarian function during laparoscopic ovarian cystectomy? Select 2 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Use of monopolar energy for cortical incision away from hilum
C) Minimal electrosurgical use on the cyst bed
D) En bloc resection of ovary
E) Leaving part of cyst wall to avoid bleeding

questions.explanation: Hydrodissection gently separates the cyst wall; minimizing energy on the bed preserves ovarian reserve. En bloc resection sacrifices tissue; leaving wall risks recurrence.
questions.submodule: Module 8.3 – Laparoscopic Treatment of an Adnexal Mass

questions.text: Which of the following steps help prevent ureteral injury during laparoscopic oophorectomy? Select best 2 answers.

answerOptions.text: C) Clamping and cutting IP ligament near ovary
E) Entry into retroperitoneum medial to IP ligament

questions.explanation: Skeletonize the IP and identify the ureter retroperitoneally to keep dissection safe. Transection should be away from the ovary after confirming ureter location; lateral entry is preferred.

questions.text: Which of the following are not appropriate surgical approaches to suspected ovarian torsion in reproductive-age women? Select all that are incorrect.

answerOptions.text: B) Oophorectomy in all torsion cases
D) Suspension to reduce recurrence risk
E) Expectant management for classic ultrasound findings

questions.explanation: Detorsion with ovarian conservation is preferred; oophorectomy only if clearly nonviable. Expectant management risks ischemic damage; suspension can reduce recurrence.

questions.text: What is the most appropriate first tool for lysing filmy, delicate, and avascular adhesions?
questions.explanation: Gentle blunt traction–countertraction separates filmy adhesions while avoiding thermal injury.

questions.text: Which of the following is the preferred technique for lysing dense adhesions near the bowel?

answerOptions.text: B) Sharp dissection with laparoscopic scissors

questions.explanation: Sharp dissection reduces thermal spread and visceral injury risk when working near bowel.

questions.text: What is the initial recommended dissection approach for an obliterated anterior cul-de-sac?

answerOptions.text: A) Midline dissection from superior to inferior
B) Start at the uterine fundus and move posteriorly
D) Lateral dissection to find preserved planes
E) Perform blunt entry with a trocar

questions.explanation: Lateral entry is more likely to encounter preserved tissue planes and avoid visceral injury.

questions.text: What is the most appropriate surgical management for a patient with a ruptured ectopic pregnancy?
questions.explanation: Hemodynamic concern/hemoperitoneum requires urgent operative management.
questions.submodule: Module 8.2 – Laparoscopic Treatment of Ectopic Pregnancy

questions.text: What is the main indication for choosing a salpingostomy over a salpingectomy?

answerOptions.text: B) History of pelvic inflammatory disease

questions.explanation: When the opposite tube is compromised, salpingostomy preserves fertility.
questions.submodule: Module 8.2 – Laparoscopic Treatment of Ectopic Pregnancy

questions.text: After a salpingostomy is performed, what is the next required step regardless of pathology findings?
questions.explanation: Serial β-hCG tracks for persistent trophoblastic tissue (risk up to ~20%).
questions.submodule: Module 8.2 – Laparoscopic Treatment of Ectopic Pregnancy

questions.text: What technique is commonly used to separate an ovarian dermoid cyst from surrounding ovarian tissue?
questions.explanation: Hydrodissection facilitates gentle separation while preserving cortex.
questions.submodule: Module 8.3 – Laparoscopic Treatment of an Adnexal Mass

questions.text: Why is complete removal of the cyst wall critical in ovarian cystectomy?

answerOptions.text: A) To allow easier specimen extraction
B) To minimize need for electrosurgery

questions.explanation: Residual wall increases recurrence (e.g., dermoids, endometriomas).
questions.submodule: Module 8.3 – Laparoscopic Treatment of an Adnexal Mass

questions.text: What is the least essential step of laparoscopic ovarian cystectomy?

answerOptions.text: A) Removal of the cyst wall
B) Hemostasis of the cyst bed
E) Separation of the cyst from ovarian cortex

questions.explanation: Reconstruction isn’t always required; many incisions heal without suturing.
questions.submodule: Module 8.3 – Laparoscopic Treatment of an Adnexal Mass

questions.text: Which of the following is a critical structure to identify during laparoscopic oophorectomy?
questions.explanation: The ureter courses near the IP ligament—identify it to avoid injury.

questions.text: What is a key advantage of skeletonizing the IP ligament and identifying the ureter retroperitoneally during oophorectomy?

answerOptions.text: E) Improves visualization of fallopian tube

questions.explanation: Clear anatomy reduces risk of leaving residual ovarian tissue.

questions.text: Transecting the infundibulopelvic ligament close to the ovary increases the risk of:

answerOptions.text: A) Incomplete excision and ovarian remnant

questions.explanation: Cutting too close risks leaving ovarian tissue → ovarian remnant syndrome.

questions.text: What is the preferred initial management of suspected ovarian torsion?
questions.explanation: Timely laparoscopy for detorsion preserves ovarian function.

questions.text: What is a common ultrasound finding in ovarian torsion?

answerOptions.text: D) Follicles clustered in the center
E) Peripheralized follicles with heterogeneous stroma

questions.explanation: Typical findings: enlarged ovary, heterogeneous stroma, peripheral follicles; Doppler may be normal.

questions.text: Which of the following is a surgical method to prevent recurrence of ovarian torsion?
questions.explanation: Ovarian suspension or ligament plication strategies can reduce recurrence; suspension is the direct option here.
